messages.content: You adjudicate whether found evidence supports, contradicts, or is silent on a market-risk hypothesis. Every stance needs a VERBATIM quote from the result text. Status: 'corroborated' = ≥1 independent source materially supports the mechanism's key premise; 'refuted' = a LOAD-BEARING premise is directly contradicted by a strong source; 'contested' = credible contradiction of a non-load-bearing link; 'speculative' = no signal either way (absence of coverage is NOT refutation). You may revise the likelihood in a bounded way with a rationale; you may NOT change the perturbation or target nodes.

 "title": "The 0.40 'large operators buy direct' share is one misread order-book stat — and the corpus says the real large-operator volume flows through ODM white-label the entrant can't intercept",
 "narrative": "The venture's stated go-to-market is 'large operators first', and the model rewards that with a 0.40 customer-type slice. Meanwhile the same digest documents the actual structure: hyperscale and large-colo procurement increasingly runs through Taiwanese ODM/white-label programs (Digipower, GETEKnet) and master distributors — spend that is nominally 'large operator' but contractually locked into OEM design-win cycles a pre-launch EU brand cannot enter in Year 1. # OEM Rack PDU Solutions: Smart Power Distribution Unit for Global Data Centers

## What Is a PDU?

A Power Distribution Unit (PDU) is a critical device for distributing reliable electricity to multiple IT or telecom equipment within a rack. From basic rack PDU to intelligent PDU, As an experienced OEM manufacturer and supplier, GETEKnet provide a wide range of options to meet data center and enterprise power needs.

## Major Types of PDUs

Basic PDUs deliver straightforward power distribution.

Metered PDUs show real-time load on a display.

Switched or Managed PDUs allow remote outlet control.

Intelligent PDUs (Smart PDUs) integrate monitoring, metering, and network communication for advanced power management.

## Intelligent PDU Features

Smart PDUs support outlet-level metering, environmental sensors (temperature, humidity, airflow), and remote management through SNMP or REST API. These functions enable energy efficiency, capacity planning, and secure power distribution across global facilities.

## Plug Types and International Standards

Rack PDUs are available with a variety of plug and socket combinations to match local requirements. Besides the common IEC C13/C14/C19/C20 outlets, OEM PDUs can be built with:

NEMA connectors for the U.S. and Canada (e.g. NEMA 5-15R, 5-20R, L6-30R).

Schuko (Type F) “German” sockets widely used across Europe.

    "url": "https://www.schleifenbauer.eu/en/hib-pdu/",
    "publishedDate": "2025-10-16T12:06:33.000Z",
    "text": "HIB 2.0, HIB 2.1 en HIB 3.0 PDUs for Government Data Centres

# HIB 2.0, HIB 2.1 en HIB 3.0 PDUs

Schleifenbauer PDUs: Designed in Accordance with the Dutch Government’s HIB Standard! The Dutch government sets strict requirements for ICT infrastructure within government offices and data centres. These requirements are defined in the Handbook for ICT Infrastructure and Cabling (HIB) issued by the Government Real Estate Agency (Rijksvastgoedbedrijf, RVB). Schleifenbauer supplies Power Distribution Units (PDUs) that fully comply with these HIB standards, covering electrical safety, power distribution, surge protection, and outlet-level configuration.

Schleifenbauer PDU

## What Is HIB?

The Handbook for ICT Infrastructure and Cabling (HIB) defines the standards for passive and active ICT infrastructure in government buildings.

Its goal: to create a future-proof, secure, and standardised ICT environment for all Dutch government agencies.

HIB specifies, among other things:

• The design of ICT rooms (EF, BD, FD);

• Requirements for electrical power and PDUs;

• Fire safety, physical security, and information security (in line with NKBR and Vir-BI);

• Earthing, bonding, and surge protection (according to NEN-EN-IEC 62305-4);

• Quality standards for measurement, protection, and documentation.

For government offices and data centres, the HIB is an official standard: the infrastructure must demonstrably comply with its guidelines.

# A new dawn for industrial channels: Meeting customers where they want
September 22, 2022| Article
The time is ripe for industrial OEMs to rethink their long-held distribution model and rebalance their channel mix.
Chris Angevine[Ben Ellencweig](https://www.mckinsey.com/our-people/ben-ellencweig)Aritra GuptaDanish Shafi
### [(7 pages)](<#/download//~/media/mckinsey/industries/advanced electronics/our insights/a new dawn for industrial channels meeting customers where they want/a-new-dawn-for-industrial-channels-meeting-customers-where-they-want-f.pdf?shouldIndex=false>)
**Across a range of industrial markets,**from semiconductors to industrial automation com­ponents, OEMs have long relied on independent distributors for their go-to-market (GTM) strategies. Distributors have traditionally helped OEMs access a fragmented customer base, hold inventory as needed, manage working capital, aggregate products for end customers, and offer additional services (such as training, product support, and aftermarket services) to customers. As the eyes and ears for their respective OEMs, distributors have often been in the best position to understand what end customers needed and wanted—and how to best deliver it.

    "title": "OFCI vs CFCI: Data Center Equipment Guide",
    "url": "https://www.irecruit.co/insights/ofci-vs-cfci-procurement-owner-vs-contractor-furnished-equipment-data-centers",
    "publishedDate": "2026-06-10T00:18:09.000Z",
    "text": "OFCI vs CFCI: Data Center Equipment Guide

June 10, 2026

# OFCI vs. CFCI Procurement: Owner-Furnished vs. Contractor-Furnished Equipment on Data Centers

By:

Choosing between OFCI (Owner-Furnished, Contractor-Installed) and CFCI (Contractor-Furnished, Contractor-Installed) can significantly impact your data center project's cost, timeline, and risk.

Here’s the key difference:

- OFCI: The owner buys critical equipment (e.g., generators, switchgear) and the contractor installs it.
- CFCI: The contractor handles both procurement and installation, offering a single point of responsibility.

Key Takeaways:

- OFCI Pros: Greater control over long-lead procurement, cost transparency, and bulk purchasing for standardization.
- OFCI Cons: requires specialized construction management expertise for procurement, logistics, and risk management.
- CFCI Pros: Simplifies management for owners, consolidates responsibility with contractors, and reduces administrative burden.
- CFCI Cons: Limited owner visibility into costs and supplier choices, potential for contractor markups.

Which is better?

- Use OFCI for long-lead, high-value equipment when you have the internal resources to manage procurement.
- Opt for CFCI when you want a simpler process and can rely on the contractor's expertise.

Quick Comparison Table:

| Factor | OFCI: Owner-Furnished | CFCI: Contractor-Furnished |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Cost Transparency | High (direct vendor pricing) | Low (contractor markups included) |

    "text": "Power Distribution Unit (PDU) in the AI Era: Strategic Shifts, Supply Chain Risks, and OEM Imperatives

- Contact Us

# Power Distribution Units in the Age of Rack Densification: Strategic Implications for PDU OEMs

Article

- January 8, 2026

##### Share

## Executive Summary

In an era defined by exponential data growth and AI-driven workloads, Power Distribution Units (PDUs) have evolved from mere power strips to critical enablers of operational efficiency, risk mitigation, and sustainability in data centers. As global data center energy demands are projected to double by 2030 due to AI proliferation, investing in advanced PDUs particularly intelligent variants offers substantial returns through energy optimization, reduced downtime, and enhanced scalability.

PDUs serve as the backbone of electrical power delivery in modern data centers, distributing utility or uninterruptible power supply (UPS) feeds to racks of servers, networking equipment, and storage systems. Unlike consumer-grade power strips, PDUs are engineered for high-density environments, offering robust surge protection, circuit breakers, and compatibility with single-phase or three-phase inputs. They form the final link in the data center power chain from utility transformers to UPS systems, floor-mounted PDUs, and ultimately rack-mounted units that connect directly to IT loads.

With the rise of hyperscale and edge computing, PDUs must handle increasing rack densities, often ex"

Global Forecast to 2032"},{"url":"https://www.indexbox.io/store/european-union-rack-pdus-market-analysis-forecast-size-trends-and-insights/","stance":"silent","quote":"The European Union Rack Power Distribution Unit (PDU) market is a critical infrastructure component underpinning the bloc's accelerating digital transformation."},{"url":"https://op.europa.eu/en/web/public-procurement/procurement-details/-/procurement/551e77fc-d5d0-4e45-9725-92c69994fb45","stance":"silent","quote":"Product framework agreement -24 months Data center equipment -8 lots"}],"status":"contested","statusRationale":"The propagation premise — that ODM/white-label programs (GETEKnet, Digipower) and private-label distribution are real large-operator supply channels — is directly corroborated: GETEKnet explicitly markets 'OEM Rack PDU Solutions' serving global data centers, Digipower states it 'serves infrastructure operators and OEM partners globally' via 'OEM/ODM design expertise,' and the PDU procurement trend piece lists 'Private Label' and 'ODM Capability' as buyer sourcing criteria. This materially supports the mechanism's structural claim. However, the load-bearing quantitative premise (0.40 rests on a single misused datapoint; the direct-branded slice should shrink to ~0.27) cannot be validated — no source publishes a numeric buyer-channel split, and the McKinsey source contradicts a non-load-bearing sub-link by documenting a countervailing trend of large customers 'seeking to engage directly with industrial OEMs,' which cuts against the assumption that large-operator spend is locked away from direct channels. The falsifier (structured CE operator/distributor interviews showing ≥35% direct branded purchase) is neither confirmed nor refuted by these corpus-level sources.","evidence":[{"title":"OEM Rack PDU Solutions: Smart Power Distribution Unit for Global Data Centers","sourceType":"industry-report","publisher":"GETEKnet","date":"2025-09-10","excerpt":"As an experienced OEM manufacturer and supplier, GETEKnet provide a wide range of options to meet data center and enterprise power needs.","url":"https://www.getek.com/oem-rack-pdu-solutions-smart-power-distribution-unit-for-global-data-centers/"},{"title":"Digipower Advanced Rack PDU Solutions","sourceType":"industry-report","publisher":"Digipower Manufacturing Inc.","date":null,"excerpt":"Our comprehensive PDU portfolio serves infrastructure operators and OEM partners globally. We combine OEM/ODM design expertise with proven quality assurance.","url":"https://www.digipower.com.tw/"},{"title":"A new dawn for industrial channels: Meeting customers where they want","sourceType":"analyst-estimate","publisher":"McKinsey & Company","date":"2022-09-22","excerpt":"Large and sophisticated B2B end customers are seeking to engage directly with industrial OEMs, a trend that [is reshaping channel mix].","url":"https://www.mckinsey.com/industries/industrials-and-electronics/our-insights/a-new-dawn-for-industrial-channels-meeting-customers-where-they-want"}],"revisedLikelihood":0.42,"revisionRationale":"The qualitative existence of ODM/white-label and private-label large-operator supply channels is well corroborated (GETEKnet, Digipower, PDU sourcing trend piece), strengthening the structural mechanism. But no source supplies any numeric channel split to validate the specific 0.40→0.27 magnitude claim, and the McKinsey source documents a countervailing trend of large B2B buyers moving toward direct OEM engagement — mildly undercutting the assumption that large-operator spend is contractually unreachable. Net effect is roughly neutral, so likelihood is held near the prior with a slight downward nudge for the unresolved magnitude and the countervailing direct-engagement signal."}
